Job Title: Program Manager - Growth and Operational Excellence
Ad Text:
Company Description

Ramsay provides high quality healthcare to millions of people each year through a network of hospitals, clinics and services in Australia, Europe and the United Kingdom. 

Job Description

Program Manager – Growth & Operational Excellence 

This role can be based in Sydney (St Leonards), Melbourne (St Kilda) or Brisbane, but a flexible approach to WFH is supported. 

 

Job Summary:

You will report to the Director, Growth & Operational Excellence, this pivotal role offers an exceptional opportunity for a Program Manager to serve as a key strategic partner within our Growth & Operational Excellence team.

You will support the delivery of high-impact, data-driven performance improvement initiatives, ensuring initiatives align seamlessly with our strategic objectives, resolving roadblocks, and keeping the team on track.

This role requires a proactive leader who can anticipate challenges, facilitate executive-level collaboration, and translate strategic vision into actionable programs.

 

Key Responsibilities:

Program Leadership: Coordinate and facilitate the design and implementation of multiple programs of work, ensuring direct alignment with our strategic objectives and delivering tangible, high-impact outcomes across the organisation. This includes planning and leading detailed quarterly planning sessions, preparing for Quarterly Business Reviews, and ensuring structures and ways of working are set up to deliver on planned outcomes.

Strategic Reporting & Insights: Design and prepare executive-level presentations covering program progress, outcomes, and decisions required to senior executive management, providing data-driven recommendations that inform strategic decision-making.

Strategic Communication & Stakeholder Engagement: Cultivate clear, concise, and impactful communication across all levels, from team members to site executive leadership to the Australian senior executive team. You will drive consensus and foster productive relationships, acting as a critical interface, translating executive priorities into a clear program plan and facilitating seamless cross-functional collaboration.

Proactive Risk Mitigation: Anticipate potential program obstacles and proactively develop robust contingency plans, ensuring the smooth progression and successful delivery of initiatives.

Performance Insight & Optimisation: Maintain and improve on existing frameworks to monitor program effectiveness and delivery. You will proactively identify areas for improvement, guiding necessary adjustments and resolutions.

Budget & Resource Stewardship: Develop and manage program budgets, including forecasting, tracking expenses, and strategically reallocating resources to maximise program effectiveness and efficiency.

 

Qualifications:

Education: A Bachelor’s degree in Healthcare Management, Business Administration, or a related field is essential.

Experience: A minimum of 5 years of demonstrated experience in program management, strategic consulting, or a related role, with a proven track record of delivering complex, high-impact projects. Experience related to the health sector is preferred.

Skills: Exceptional leadership, strategic communication, and organisational skills. Advanced proficiency in data analysis, business intelligence tools, and project/program management software. Ability to influence without direct authority.

 

Personal Attributes:

Strategic & Results-Oriented: Possesses a keen strategic mindset with an unwavering focus on delivering high-quality, measurable outcomes that advance organisational goals.

Adaptable & Resilient: Thrives in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, demonstrating exceptional adaptability and resilience when navigating complex challenges.

Proactive Problem Solver: A proactive approach to identifying challenges and an innate ability to develop practical, innovative solutions.

Trusted Advisor: Builds strong relationships based on trust and credibility, acting as a reliable advisor to senior leadership and cross-functional teams.

High Integrity & Professionalism: Upholds the highest standards of integrity, professionalism, and ethical conduct.

 

About the Growth & Operational Excellence Team

Ramsay’s Growth & Operational Excellence team has built a strong reputation across the business for identifying and implementing practical, high-impact, and data-driven performance improvements through close collaboration with Operations Executive Teams. We combine an extensive range of skills and experience from both within and outside the business, and we pride ourselves on being dynamic, constructive, and collaborative.

This opportunity is ideal for a motivated and experienced Program Manager ready to help address the most pressing operational challenges and opportunities in the business. We are seeking someone who is passionate about using their strategic problem-solving acumen, analytical mindset, and strong relationship-building toolkit to support our hospitals to deliver high-value healthcare.

 

What’s in it for You:

At Ramsay, we care for our people by offering benefits that truly support your career, wellbeing and lifestyle, including:

Grow your career with recognition programs, employee referral bonuses, scholarships, free access to LinkedIn Learning and the Ramsay Leadership Academy
Save more every day with Ramsay Rewards, giving you exclusive deals at 100+ retailers, restaurants, travel providers and more
Enjoy peace of mind with lower hospital cover costs and higher extras limits on services like General and Major Dental, plus pharmacy and allied health discounts
Find balance through flexible rostering options, flexible leave and access to free, confidential counselling and wellbeing support 24/7
Increase your take-home pay through salary packaging and novated leasing, plus discounts on leisure travel, gyms and lifestyle services.

 

Why Join Ramsay:

At Ramsay Health Care, “people caring for people” is at the heart of everything we do. We are:

Australia’s largest private hospital operator, with over 74 sites and 35,000 staff
A values-led workplace where you’ll be supported personally and professionally
A place with flexible, permanent and career-growth opportunities nationwide.
Additional Information

You must provide or complete a National Police Check conducted within the previous 12 months.

Apply:

Ready to feel cared for while you care for others? Apply today. All applications must be lodged online.

Applications close: 28 April 2026

Reference number: JR111713

Please Note:

Ramsay Health Care is committed to Child Safety. Details of our commitment are set out in our Code of Conduct, available at www.ramsayhealth.com.au.

Applications made by recruitment agencies will not be considered.

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

As SEEK’s Strategy Principal, you’ll play a key role in defining and delivering our long-term commercial vision. Reporting to the APAC Director of Strategy, you’ll lead cross-functional projects that shape SEEK’s marketplace strategy across Asia-Pacific. You’ll guide teams, influence stakeholders and turn complex data into clear insights that drive executive decision-making. With a strong focus on collaboration, you’ll bridge strategy and execution — ensuring SEEK’s bold ambitions translate into meaningful outcomes for our customers, partners and community.

You will:

own delivery of commercial strategy development from framing problems to presenting solutions;
collaborate across Product, Commercial, AI, Finance, Marketing and Country Leadership teams;
build deep expertise in market trends, customer needs and competitive dynamics;
communicate strategy clearly and effectively at all levels of SEEK;
coach and mentor team members to grow capability and performance;
drive continuous improvement in strategy operating models and ways of working; and
represent Commercial Strategy in leadership forums and strategic initiatives.

Key responsibilities

Lead strategic projects that shape SEEK’s long-term vision.
Frame problems, analyse data and build actionable insights.
Partner with business leaders to prioritise and execute strategic initiatives.
Simplify complex narratives into clear recommendations for executives.
Mentor and develop team members for high performance.
Lead cross-functional collaboration across Product, Commercial and regional teams.
Track market trends and translate insights into thought leadership.

The Team

SEEK global Strategy team is composed of ~30 mostly ex-consultants from top-tier consulting firms. We offer an intellectually stimulating environment, a great team with a caring heart, a fun and inspiring environment and a great purpose to wake up to every morning. You will work closely and interact on a regular basis with:

Director of Strategy, Commercial
Director of Strategy, Product
Group Executive, Commercial
Group Executive, Product
Directors of the Commercial Leadership team
Other members of the Strategy Leadership Team
Other members of the Executive team on an ad-hoc basis
Other members of the APAC SEEK strategy team
Qualifications
8+ years' experience in strategy roles, including 5+ years in top-tier consulting, PE or investment banking.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Proven success leading strategic projects from concept to delivery.
Experience influencing senior stakeholders across functions and markets.
Demonstrated people management and coaching experience.
Deep interest in technology, online marketplaces and innovation cultures.
Additional Information

At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year
Free income protection insurance

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

We are seeking a Senior Data Architect for a fixed 3-month contract with deep, hands-on experience designing and delivering Medallion Architecture and Customer 360 platforms. This is a technically demanding role that requires someone who has personally built unified customer data models, identity resolution layers and production-grade lakehouse solutions at enterprise scale, not simply designed them on paper. 




Responsibilities:

Design the architecture and hands-on implementation of a Medallion architecture 
Design source-to-target mappings, data models, transformation logic and business rules for each layer of the medallion architecture. 
Implement identity resolution and crosswalk logic that links customer identifiers across different systems. 
Define semantic-layer business logic, governed metrics and KPI definitions for downstream analytics consumption. 
Productionize the end-to-end solution with data-quality checks, monitoring, orchestration and documentation. 
Partner with business stakeholders across marketing, operations, product and technology to translate requirements into technical delivery. 
Provide build ready requirements and artifacts to data engineers and analysts on medallion best practices, identity resolution patterns and data quality standards. 



Experience:

Proven delivery of enterprise-scale Medallion Architecture, including Silver and Gold data models for business-ready analytics
Hands-on experience taking data platforms from development to production, with strong quality, monitoring and operational standards
Direct experience building customer identity resolution / crosswalk solutions, including deterministic and probabilistic matching
Track record delivering a Customer 360 / single customer view, integrating CRM, digital, contact-centre and transactional data
Strong capability in customer-centric data modelling, semantic layers and governed metrics as a single source of truth
Experience modelling digital, behavioural and contact-centre data (e.g. GA4, Adobe, Genesys or similar)
Advanced skills in Python, SQL and modern lakehouse platforms (Microsoft Fabric, Databricks or equivalent)
Experience building automated, production-grade data pipelines with orchestration, data quality and governance controls
Expertise in Power BI semantic models and executive reporting
Desirable: experience in insurance or financial services, customer analytics or NLP/text analytics use cases

Company description:


Shape growth strategy, new ventures and AI-enabled investment decisions across Kleenheat and WesCEF
Build a long-term career with WesCEF — competitive salary, share allocations, and opportunities across the Wesfarmers Group
Permanent, full-time role based at our new Perth CBD office.

Kleenheat is a trusted Western Australian energy brand with more than 65 years of history, supplying natural gas and electricity to homes and businesses across WA. As part of WesCEF and the broader Wesfarmers Group, Kleenheat is well positioned for future growth across energy retail, solar, batteries and emerging technologies.

We are seeking a Senior Commercial Analyst to play a high‑impact role across Kleenheat's Natural Gas & Electricity (NG&E) business and AI‑enabled initiatives across WesCEF.

This is an opportunity for a commercially minded analyst who wants exposure to growth initiatives, new ventures and enterprise‑scale decision making, while building a long‑term career within one of Australia's most successful diversified groups.

We pride ourselves on our values of One Team, Accountable, Customer Focused and Forward Thinking, with the understanding that our success is underpinned by our people.



Job description:



About the role

As a Senior Commercial Analyst, you will play a high‑impact role across Kleenheat's Natural Gas & Electricity business and AI‑enabled initiatives across WesCEF. You will support strategic growth initiatives, new ventures and emerging energy solutions, providing the commercial insight and rigour that underpins high‑value investment and prioritisation decisions.

Partnering closely with stakeholders across Commercial, Business Development, Digital and IT, you will translate complex modelling, customer economics and AI use cases into clear actionable insights. You will influence investment decisions, optimise growth initiatives and provide insight on commercial performance, risks and trade-offs.

Key Responsibilities:

Growth & strategic initiatives

Support and shape growth initiatives, including digital, AI‑enabled, and distributed energy solutions.
Provide commercial support to Kleenheat Spark ventures, from opportunity assessment through to performance tracking.
Translate emerging WesCEF AI use cases into tangible commercial value, focusing on revenue uplift, cost efficiency, and customer outcomes.

Commercial analytics & insights

Develop and own commercial models, forecasts, and business cases to support investment and prioritisation decisions.
Analyse customer behaviour, pricing, offers, and lifecycle value to inform growth and retention strategies.
Identify risks and opportunities across growth initiatives and provide clear, actionable recommendations to stakeholders.

Stakeholder engagement

Partner with teams across Commercial, Business Development, Digital, and IT within Kleenheat and the broader WesCEF group.
Present insights and recommendations to senior leadership in a clear, commercially focused way.
Act as a trusted advisor on commercial performance, trade‑offs, and growth levers.

About You

Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Commerce, Economics, Finance (or a closely related discipline)
Strong experience in commercial analysis, strategy, or business analytics, ideally in energy, utilities, or complex consumer environments.
Demonstrated ability to support new products, ventures, or growth initiatives from concept through to execution.
Strong financial and commercial modelling capability (business cases, ROI, scenario analysis).
Comfortable working with large datasets, dashboards, and analytics tools (e.g. Power BI).
Curiosity and capability to work with AI‑enabled insights and emerging digital platforms.
Strong stakeholder management skills, with the confidence to challenge and influence.
Comfortable operating in ambiguous, fast‑moving environments typical of innovation and growth initiatives.
Clear communicator who can simplify complexity for non‑technical audiences.

What we offer

At Wesfarmers Chemicals, Energy & Fertilisers (WesCEF), we value our people and offer a range of benefits designed to support your wellbeing, development and career growth, including:

Competitive remuneration, including access to the Wesfarmers employee share plan and participation in our annual incentive plan
Flexible working arrangements, including some work-from-home options where applicable
Salary sacrifice opportunities, including novated leasing
Access to a wide range of employee benefits across the Wesfarmers Group, including staff discounts at Bunnings, Kmart, Target, Officeworks and Kleenheat, as well as access to Wesfarmers-sponsored arts and cultural events
Paid parental leave of up to 18 weeks
Ongoing professional development and career opportunities across WesCEF and the broader Wesfarmers Group

Why WesCEF

At WesCEF, we are committed to building an engaged, high-performing and inclusive workforce that reflects the communities in which we operate. We value diversity of thinking, skills and experience, knowing this drives innovation and better business outcomes.

We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds and encourage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples to apply.

We are proud to support flexible working arrangements and understand that flexibility looks different for everyone. If flexibility is important to you, we encourage you to discuss your needs as part of the application process.

WesCEF operates across multiple locations, with our two major hubs based in Kwinana and the Perth CBD.

Applications close: Sunday 3rd May 2026

Our recruitment process considers your alignment to our company values and the requirements of the role. We also have several checks which may be conducted to demonstrate your suitability for the role including but not limited to psychometric assessment, police checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, right to work check, qualifications, due diligence and/or reference checks.    

We reserve the right to commence the recruitment process prior to the closing date. Therefore, it is recommended to apply as soon as possible.    

Agency applications will not be accepted.

Head of Data & AI | Sydney | ASX Listed Digital Financial Services

The opportunity
A high growth, ASX listed digital financial services business is looking for a Head of Data & AI to lead its next chapter of innovation across agentic AI, machine learning, and enterprise data strategy.

This is a rare leadership role at the intersection of AI vision and execution. You'll own the end-to-end data and AI strategy, from data foundations and advanced analytics through to generative AI and agentic systems.

What makes this exciting
This isn't a "build the strategy and hand it off" role. You'll lead high performing engineering teams, stand up agentic infrastructure, drive AI into products and operations, and shape what the next generation hybrid workforce looks like in a regulated financial services environment. The business is profitable, ambitious, and ready to move fast.

You will have a genuine mandate to industrialise AI across the enterprise not just experiment with it.

What you'll do
Define and execute the Data & AI strategy across engineering, ML platforms, and agentic operations. Build and scale AI powered products and services for customers and merchants. Establish responsible AI governance and frameworks that balance innovation with trust and compliance. Lead, grow, and inspire a team of data engineers, ML engineers, and AI specialists.

What you'll bring

15+ years in AI, Data, or ML Engineering leadership, with at least 5 years in senior executive roles.
A graduate degree in AI, Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering or related discipline.
Proven experience delivering large scale AI and data initiatives in financial services or other regulated industries.
Hands on credibility across data platforms, ML/AI tooling, model governance, and cloud native architecture.
Direct experience with agentic AI, multi agent systems, or intelligent automation is highly desirable.
The ability to translate complex technical capability into tangible business value and to bring the boardroom along with you.
Why move?

A genuine leadership seat with enterprise-wide influence.
A business that's evolved into a broader digital payments and commerce platform, operating at significant scale across multiple markets.
The chance to build something new - agentic operations, AI native products, a world class data and AI function - rather than maintain what already exists.
To apply, please lodge your application via the link in this advertisement. For specific enquiries, please contact Katharine Whittaker on kwhittaker@derwentsearch.com.au referencing 33865.

Applications close: 27th April 2026

Are you the person in the room who actually knows how a profit centre ticks — and can translate that into a system requirement?

We're looking for a senior Business Systems Analyst who has walked the floor of an electrical trade or wholesale business, understands the systems that make it run, and can bridge the gap between operations and IT.

The Company

MM Electrical (MMEM) is one of Australia's largest electrical wholesale and trade retail networks. Our Business Systems and Support team keeps the technology engine running — from ERP to EDI to integrations — across a national network of branches and profit centres.

The Role

This is a newly created senior position that sits at the intersection of L2 support and business analysis — a genuine 50/50 split. You'll report directly to the Head of Business Systems and Support and work alongside a tight-knit, highly capable team including our senior BA, L1 and L2 support teams and in-house developer.

There's no hand-holding here — we want someone who can pick up a complex support ticket and drive it to resolution in the morning, then sit in a requirements workshop with the Finance team in the afternoon.

What You'll Be Doing

• Business Analysis: requirements elicitation, process mapping, solution design, and translating business needs into clear specs for our developer.

• L2 Support: owning escalated issues across Unibis ERP, EDI (B2BE), and integrated finance and reporting systems.

• Working with our in-house developer to test and deliver system enhancements.

• Engaging Finance, operations, and branch stakeholders to gather insights and drive improvements.

• Contributing to knowledge base documentation and the continuous improvement of our support capability.

What We're Looking For

• 5+ years in a Business Systems Analyst, IT Systems Analyst, or equivalent role — you've done this before.

• Real-world experience inside an electrical trade retail or wholesale business — think Middy's, Rexel, Voltex, or similar.

• You know how a profit centre operates end-to-end: POS, purchasing, inventory, branch accounting, customer accounts.

• You can write a proper requirements document AND work a support ticket through to resolution — both with equal professionalism.

• Unibis ERP experience is a genuine advantage, but if you have the domain knowledge and the BA skills, we'll teach you the platform.

• EDI experience (B2BE or equivalent) is a bonus.

• You're autonomous, comfortable working hybrid, and you communicate clearly with everyone from the branch manager to the CFO.

The Details

• Salary: $130,000 – $160,000 + superannuation (depending on experience)

• Working arrangement: Hybrid — Currently 3 days North Rocks office / 2 days WFH

• Employment type: Permanent, full-time

• Location: North Rocks, NSW (must be within reasonable commute)

• Australian work rights are required for this position.

If you've spent years working with electrical trade systems and you're ready to bring that knowledge into a head office IT role with real scope, we'd love to hear from you.

Apply now with your CV and a Cover Letter outlining your experience within the electrical trade industry and the business sytems you've worked with.

Applicants who fail to submit a cover letter will not be considered for this role.

Ad Text:
Drive enterprise service delivery in support of front line services?
Full time permanent role with flexible working arrangements
Brisbane, Melbourne or Canberra

The Organisation  

Airservices Australia is a government-owned organisation responsible for the safe and efficient management of 11% of the world’s airspace and provision of air traffic services at Australia’s busiest airports.

Our people are our greatest asset with a dynamic and diverse team operating from locations across the country - from bustling cities to regional and remote locations. This team keep’s Australia’s aviation industry safe every day of the year, both in the air and on the ground.

We connect people with their world safely – linking family and friends, generating economic activity, creating jobs, and facilitating trade and tourism. 

Airservices is committed to fostering a culture that is diverse, inclusive, and respectful.  We encourage motivated individuals who love what they do, value a service first mindset, and embrace a challenge to explore a career with Airservices. In return you will be a valued team member, be offered flexibility and experience a meaningful career in an exciting, ever-evolving aviation industry. 

The Role & The Team

Reporting to the Head of Enabling Technology Enablement, you will lead key enterprise platforms that help Airservices run smoothly and deliver critical services across the organisation.

You will shape platform strategy and roadmaps, lead digitalisation and automation initiatives, uplift data and analytics capability, and ensure enterprise applications operate reliably, securely and efficiently. This role partners closely with technology leaders, executives, and external vendors to deliver modern, scalable and integrated platforms that enable business outcomes.

You will lead and develop a high-performing, geographically dispersed team and play a key role in driving cultural change, continuous improvement and new ways of working.

What you will do:

Lead and develop a high‑performing technology team, fostering a positive, inclusive and accountable culture
Own and manage enterprise applications, data and analytics platforms to ensure they are reliable and fit for purpose
Shape and deliver platform strategies, roadmaps, upgrades and continuous improvement initiatives
Drive digitalisation and automation to simplify processes and improve business outcomes
Partner with senior leaders and stakeholders as a trusted technology advisor
Oversee vendor relationships and technology initiatives from selection through to delivery and operations
Strengthen data and analytics capability to support a more data‑driven organisation
Ensure critical corporate systems operate reliably and meet service level expectations

Please click on the below link to view the PD: 

 Enterprise Platforms Applications Data Analytics.pdf

About you:

You are a collaborative, strategic technology leader who thrives in complex environments and enjoys translating business needs into practical, contemporary platform solutions.

The successful applicant will have:

• Demonstrated leadership of large enterprise application, data and analytics platforms

• Experience managing technology portfolios, budgets and vendors

• Strong capability across ERP, CRM, integration and data platforms

• Proven leadership of digitalisation and automation initiatives

• Experience leading geographically dispersed teams

• Strong senior stakeholder engagement skills

Airservices Australia is a diverse and inclusive employer that recognises the benefits of having a workforce that reflects the communities in which we work. We encourage applications from diverse groups including Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander people. 

Security Clearance 

To be eligible to apply, you must be an Australian citizen as an NV1 security clearance is required for this role. Please refer to the security check requirements successful applicants will be required to undertake as part of the recruitment process.

Should you require any further information please contact Neethu Stephen – Talent Advisor at Neethuanna.Stephen@airservicesaustralia.com 

How to Apply

To apply for this position please lodge an application online via the “apply link” attaching your resume and a cover letter outlining your skills and experience and how they relate to this role.

 Applications close on Wednesday April 29th 2026

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst – Retail, SAP & Automation‑Aware
Ad Text:
Since 2024, 7-Eleven Australia has joined 7-Eleven international to be part of the biggest retail network across the world, represented in 20 countries with over 84,000 stores. We have big growth plans in Australia and a lot of opportunity for someone who wants to be part of ever growing retailer with a global footprint.

Firstly, what we offer you! 

Vibrant Open Office in Richmond. Work in a dynamic, collaborative space that sparks creativity 

Work Your Way. Enjoy the perfect balance of remote flexibility and in-office collaboration—get the best of both worlds

Make a Difference. Take a paid day off each year to volunteer for a cause you’re passionate about

Fuel Your Day. Enjoy free 7-Eleven coffee and snacks in the office—because great ideas start with great coffee

Family Comes First. Get up to 15 weeks of paid parental leave for both primary and secondary carer, so you can focus on what matters most

Stay Social & Connected. Join our Social Club and Open Committee for regular events, celebrations, and fun activities

Grow Without Limits. Access unlimited LinkedIn Learning courses and invest in your personal and professional development

The Role

We are seeking a Senior Business Analyst with strong retail domain experience, solid exposure to SAP environments, and an automation‑aware mindset.  

The role operates across strategy and delivery, partnering with business leaders, technology teams, and vendors to analyse problems, shape solutions, and drive sustainable operational outcomes.

Note: This is not an automation build role. The focus is automation‑informed analysis and solution design.

Key Responsibilities

Lead end‑to‑end business analysis across retail, SAP, digital, and operational initiatives.

Conduct current‑state, future‑state, gap, and impact analysis.

Elicit, document, and manage business, functional, and non‑functional requirements.

Translate requirements into user stories, acceptance criteria, and solution artefacts.

Facilitate workshops, stakeholder discussions, and decision forums.

Support solution design, UAT, and implementation.

Maintain requirements traceability through delivery.

About You

5+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst.

Retail industry experience.

SAP and Digital experience in a business or technology context.

Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills.

Experience working with vendors and third‑party partners.

Strong analytical, communication, and problem‑solving skills.

Strong understanding of store operations, POS, pricing and promotions, inventory, supply chain, and customer journeys.

Conceptual understanding of automation approaches (e.g. Power Automate, RPA, APIs).

Experience working with external vendors and delivery partners.

Experience working with digital platforms (eCommerce, mobile, loyalty, customer platforms).

A Federal Government department is seeking multiple Senior Technical Analysts to assist in its goal of transitioning to a modern sovereign cloud environment. The Senior Technical Analysts will be responsible for mapping out the current legacy ecosystem to create a path towards the transition to the new system.

You will be responsible for understanding legacy system logic, validating technical requirements for External Costing Requests (ECRs), ensuring solution designs align to business’ requirements, and defining the data flows and integrations required for the cloud modernisation. You will work across a diverse portfolio, moving between deep-dive system analysis and high-level planning.

Contract Details:

Initial 12 month contract with 2 X 12 month extension options

Estimated start date May 2026 (pending onboarding and Availability)

Baseline Security clearance required (you must be an Australian Citizen to go through the vetting process).

Full time 40-hour work week

This role can be located in ACT, VIC or SA. Canberra location preferred

Hybrid working arrangement. Mix of onsite and WFH




Key responsibilities

Document current-state technical architectures to inform the migration strategies for the program.

Map data flows and interface specifications, as well as define technical requirements (with support from the team’s solution architecture stream).

Liaise with solution and technical architecture expertise on ECRs (External Costing Requests).

Hands-on development of technical assessments to support major capability decisions – ie, enablement of cloud, EDW migrations, solution assessments for digital/functional capabilities.

Document current-state technical architectures to inform the migration strategies for the program.

Conduct deep-dive analysis of DVA’s legacy application estate to identify dependencies and integration points.




Desired experience:

The ability to analyse data structures and map complex data migrations.

The ability to read and understand technical documentation, including API specifications, database schemas (SQL), and infrastructure diagrams. Experience with legacy mainframe or mid-range systems highly regarded.

Proven experience, breaking down complex technical problems into clear, documented requirements (e.g., Functional Specifications, Interface Control Documents).

Experience with modelling tools (e.g., Visio) and work management platforms (e.g., Jira).

Experience working with Data Visualisation tools (Power BI and Tableau).




What is on offer

12 + 12 + 12 month contract

The chance to play a key role in a long term ICT modernisation project

Flexibility with working from home and in the office




How to apply

If you would like to apply for this role please click the quick apply button or email Liam O'Meara at Liam@datadiv.com.au.

Salary: Flexible work, annual bonus, inclusive culture
Ad Text:
Here at Investa, we’re all about creating spaces that reflect how people work, live, and thrive today—and how they’ll want to do so in the future. For over 20 years, we’ve led the way in making positive change in Australia’s workplaces. We’re an Australian diversified real estate company with a recognised track-record of investment and development across both office and living markets. Our purpose is to welcome the world to better places. We’re people first and entrepreneurial, with a strong service ethos.  

Why join us? We’ve made it our business to understand how people want to live and work today, tomorrow and beyond and as you’d expect, we practise what we preach in our own workplace as well.​ We’re known for our supportive, inclusive, and flexible work culture. With recognition from the Workplace Gender Equality Agency, the Australian Workplace Equality Index, and multiple awards for workplace flexibility, our people and the way we work together are our difference. 

As our Senior Research Manager, you will play a pivotal role in shaping market insights that drive investor engagement and capital raising. Your critical analysis will influence strategic decisions and elevate the Research team’s reputation through best-practice analytics.


You’re a confident communicator who shines when presenting to senior stakeholders and investors. With a talent for writing and speaking clearly, concisely, and with impact, you bring a commercially minded, ‘investor-first’ approach to every interaction. Job Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora, 你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

Where and how you can work
Our flagship campus is in Sydney. We also have a campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide. But you have choice in where and how you work, we trust our Canvanauts to choose the balance that empowers them and their team to achieve their goals.

About the Role

Lead and contribute to the Data Platform strategy and roadmap. Working alongside engineering leadership, you'll shape the vision for how Canva's data platform evolves — prioritising investments across ingestion, transformation, orchestration, governance, and the Semantic Layer. You'll build the business cases that unlock resources and keep the platform aligned to company goals.

Chart the course for the AI first evolution of data, analytics and decision making. You'll define how Canva leverages AI to transform analytics, insights, and decision-making. This is early-stage work that needs product leadership to move from experimentation to a coherent, scalable strategy — including conversational analytics, automated decisioning, and AI-powered self-serve insights.

Build deep relationships across a complex stakeholder landscape. Your users span data scientists, analytics engineers, business teams, and company leadership. You'll maintain regular touchpoints, run forums, and build the trust needed to align competing priorities across a broad surface area.

Drive platform adoption, enablement, and measurable impact. You'll establish the measurement frameworks that track platform health, adoption, and ROI. You'll drive enablement programs that help teams self-serve, and tell the story of DPG's impact through metrics, case studies, and regular stakeholder updates.

Strengthen governance, access, and data trust. You'll ensure the platform's governance and access controls mature alongside Canva — driving improvements to data sharing, retention, security, and audit readiness in partnership with engineering, security, and legal.

You're probably a match if

This is a specialised domain role. We're looking for someone with deep experience in data platforms and a track record of leading product in technically complex, high-stakes environments.

You've managed major data platforms for significant digital organisations. You understand the full stack - from ingestion and transformation through to BI and analytics - and you're fluent in how data science and AI teams consume platform capabilities.

You've worked with Snowflake, Google Cloud/BigQuery, AWS, Databricks or equivalent platforms at scale. You understand the architecture trade-offs and can hold your own in technical conversations.

 You've led major shifts in data platform architecture and strategic direction — not just incremental improvements, but meaningful changes to how an organisation manages, governs, and leverages its data.

You've worked on or shaped AI capabilities such as personalisation, conversational analytics, automated decisioning, agentic workflows or AI-driven data products. You know what's hype and what's real.

You've navigated stakeholder groups spanning engineering, data science, product, finance, marketing, and go-to-market. You know how to build alignment across competing priorities and earn trust with deeply technical teams.

Experience with analytics and visualisation platforms (Tableau, Looker, Mode, Omni, or open-source tools), or experience navigating regulatory, compliance, or enterprise data governance requirements at scale.

About the team

The Infra SuperGroup at Canva makes complex technology simple for Canvanauts by building foundational platforms that propel Canva's growth. Every week, thousands of engineers interact with the tools, utilities, and platforms that Infra provides. Infra plays a direct role in improving developer impact across the entire software lifecycle.

Within Infra, the Data Platform Group (DPG) builds and operates the data platform so Canvanauts can self-serve reliable, governed, and performant data. DPG provides paved-road capabilities — tools, patterns, and SLO-backed services — while domain teams own their data products. The group's surface area spans ingestion, transformation, orchestration, governance, observability, activation, and BI/analytics. It's built on Snowflake, dbt, Apache Airflow, and a growing governed Semantic Layer that powers both BI and emerging AI use cases.

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

We celebrate all types of skills and backgrounds at Canva so even if you don’t feel like your skills quite match what’s listed above - we still want to hear from you!

Please note that interviews are conducted virtually.

Introduction

As a member the Promontory Australia team at IBM you will contribute to the firm's efforts in working with clients on challenging strategic, risk management, and regulatory compliance issues.

Our team of specialists offers the following services across Prudential, Conduct and AML Regulation:

· Risk Management – Providing advisory and assurance services regarding organisational transformation across risk management, governance, culture, accountability and compliance.

· Remediation Services – Assurance to regulators and firms over customer focused remediation programs.

· Regulatory Framework – Advising regulators and industry associations on building regulatory frameworks.

· Regulatory Liaison – Helping our clients navigate relationships with regulators.

· RegTech – Working with IBM teams and leveraging their technology assets in advising clients on AI based solutions and associated risk governance.

We have a diverse client base in Australia, which includes some of Australia's largest financial institutions and clients drawn from the public sector.

As a Principal, you will be responsible for managing risk, regulatory and compliance engagements on a day-to-day basis, analysing complex problems, developing recommendations to address client issues and heavily involved in reports and other deliverables. A strong consulting background with experience in non-financial risk management (including operational risk and resilience) and or superannuation is preferable. You will also be expected to draw upon your insights and networks to identify business opportunities for IBM Promontory to pursue.




Roles and Responsibilities

To be successful in this role a candidate must possess strong leadership skills and have the stature to engage effectively with clients and potential clients at all levels. The candidate will also need to inspire and work effectively with team members drawn from a range of technical disciplines. Interact comfortably with executive management across industry and regulatory authorities. Key responsibilities will include:

Manage projects and act as client point of contact on a day-to-day basis contributing to engagements being delivered to budget and meeting profit and realisation targets.

Undertake analysis as directed and take responsibility for the preparation of project deliverables, including reports and presentations.

Interact comfortably with executive management of and regulatory authorities, generally with a Managing Director present.

Strong analytical skills, oral skills, with demonstrated ability to communicate clearly, simply and logically.

Strong written communication skills with, the ability to construct well-founded, clear, and concise analyses and recommendations.

Ability to handle complex information, solve problems, and manage multiple tasks.

Ability to work closely with, and communicate effectively with, clients and within all levels of the organisation.

Strong commitment to integrity and ethics.

The candidate will also be required to build and retain relationships across the industry and to identify opportunities for further work. This includes the ability to establish and maintain positive relationships and build teams that are motivated to succeed.




Required Professional and Technical Expertise

· An undergraduate degree in either Accounting, Commerce, Finance, or Law - with postgraduate qualifications highly regarded.

· At least 6 years experience in Financial services, Consulting, Regulator, Compliance, Risk management, Governance or legal.

· Track record working in a fast-paced and unstructured environment with aptitude for identifying and developing innovative solutions to client challenges.

· Analytics and data: Proven track record of successfully delivering highly analytical and data intensive work. Strong understanding of data governance and experience in working with large and unstructured datasets to draw meaningful and actionable insights and outcomes.

· AI: A strong understanding of AI fundamentals, including AI governance, and model development and application.

· Expert level capabilities in Excel (incl. advanced excel functionality and VBA / Macro development). Expert level capabilities in working with large datasets in SQL.




Preferred Professional and Technical Expertise

· Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Finance, Law or Business Administration with postgraduate qualifications highly regarded.

· Specialising in data analytics, risk management, assurance, and regulatory compliance.

· Experience developing, testing and validating the end-to-end modelling process and knowledge of a variety of tools including Alteryx, R and Python.




About the Business Unit:

IBM Promontory, a business unit of IBM Consulting, is the premier consulting firm for financial services companies. IBM Promontory excels at helping clients resolve critical issues, particularly those with a regulatory dimension. Promontory professionals have unparalleled regulatory experience and insight, and provide our clients with frank, proactive advice informed by best practices and regulatory expectations.

Promontory Australia is a boutique operation and maintains links to the US and UK. Our consultants in Australia have diverse backgrounds from regulatory authorities, industry and consulting.

In recent years Promontory has completed a number of high profile engagements with Australian financial institutions and due to a strong pipeline of work we are looking to expand our team with a highly skilled, adaptable and dependable individual to join the high performing Sydney office.

Job Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer (Team Lead) - Various Australian Cities
Ad Text:
Opportunity available:  Head Office in Brisbane, Sydney or Melbourne 

At Flight Centre Travel Group (FCTG) our purpose is to 'open up the world for those who want to see'. Every day, we give people all around the world the opportunity to experience something amazing – travel!  

As our Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) Team Leader, you will drive the delivery of high impact, first of their kind AI solutions within the travel industry. You will lead the design and productionisation of advanced AI products that operate reliably at scale, shaping the long-term AI strategy and platform foundations.

This role spans Classical ML, Generative AI, AI agents and agentic workflows. You will define robust ML and LLM infrastructure, establish strong engineering standards across MLOps and LLMOps, and ensure production grade evaluation, safeguards and observability across all AI systems.

If you enjoy coaching and mentoring engineers, building technical depth across the team, and managing multiple concurrent AI initiatives from discovery through to large scale deployment, we encourage you to apply! 

This is a rare opportunity to lead industry defining AI capabilities and deliver advanced, scalable AI products in one of the most data rich sectors globally!

 

Day to Day

 

Lead the development and productionisation of ML models, LLM powered systems and agent based applications
Define and build end to end MLOps including CI CD, model registry, monitoring, drift detection and retraining for predictive ML systems
Establish LLMOps standards including context engineering, automated evaluation pipelines, red teaming, safeguards and policy guardrails
Architect and build AI agent workflows, endpoints, gateways and orchestration layers enabling secure tool access, structured reasoning and multi agent collaboration
Design and govern MCP servers and modern agent communication protocols to ensure interoperability, security and scalability
Implement strong observability across ML and GenAI systems including reliability, latency, evaluation metrics, usage tracking and cost control
Drive scalable ML infrastructure, feature stores and data platforms on Databricks
Oversee Kubernetes based deployments and cloud native AI infrastructure
Partner with senior stakeholders to prioritise and deliver multiple high impact AI initiatives
Coach and grow a high performing AI engineering team

 

We would love to hear from you, if you have:

 

7+ years delivering production grade ML or AI systems with proven commercial impact

Your new role

As the Financial Modeller, you will be engaged to design, build and embed robust financial models that underpin budgeting, forecasting and funding management for a high-profile, multi-year government program.

This role is based in Canberra, with flexibility considered for the right candidate.



What you'll need to succeed

To be successful in this role, you will demonstrate:

NV1 security clearance
Proven experience in financial modelling, budgeting and forecasting within government or complex organisational environments
Advanced Excel modelling capability, including multi-year forecasting, scenario and sensitivity analysis
Experience working with program-based or grant-funded budgets
Strong understanding of Australian Government financial frameworks, including appropriations and grants
Ability to convert complex financial data into clear, decision-ready insights
Strong stakeholder engagement skills and confidence working with non-finance teams

What you'll get in return

You will be engaged in a high-impact financial modelling role supporting a nationally significant program, with the opportunity to influence how financial planning and forecasting is embedded across the organisation.

They’re known for working closely with the business and asking a lot of curious questions to make sure that they have the information they need to make the solution fit for purpose. You’ll partner closely with teams across Aurora Energy to unpack complex challenges, ask curious questions, and turn ideas into ethical, fit‑for‑purpose AI solutions. You’ll help guide our AI capability and ways of working, while staying hands‑on where it adds the most value. You’re curious, collaborative and understand how to use AI to solve meaningful problems. You like working closely with the business, getting to the heart of what’s needed, and turning ideas into practical outcomes.

What’s on Offer

With a focus on innovation and delivering better customer outcomes, there has never been a better time to work in the Tasmanian energy sector with Aurora Energy. Be a part of Tasmanians’ energy future and make a strong contribution to the ongoing success of our business.

We work hard to create a workplace that is welcoming and flexible, while balancing the needs of our people, the business and our customers.

Job Title: Senior Data Scientist, Algorithm (Relo to SF)
Ad Text:
About Airwallex


Airwallex is the only unified payments and financial platform for global businesses. Powered by our unique combination of proprietary infrastructure and software, we empower over 200,000 businesses worldwide – including Brex, Rippling, Navan, Qantas, SHEIN and many more – with fully integrated solutions to manage everything from business accounts, payments, spend management and treasury, to embedded finance at a global scale.

Proudly founded in Melbourne, we have a team of over 2,000 of the brightest and most innovative people in tech across 26 offices around the globe. Valued at US$8 billion and backed by world-leading investors including T. Rowe Price, Visa, Mastercard, Robinhood Ventures, Sequoia, Salesforce Ventures, DST Global, and Lone Pine Capital, Airwallex is leading the charge in building the global payments and financial platform of the future. If you’re ready to do the most ambitious work of your career, join us.

Attributes We Value


We hire successful builders with founder-like energy who want real impact, accelerated learning, and true ownership. You bring strong role-related expertise and sharp thinking, and you’re motivated by our mission and operating principles. You move fast with good judgment, dig deep with curiosity, and make decisions from first principles, balancing speed and rigor.

You’re humble and collaborative; turn zero‑to‑one ideas into real products, and you “get stuff done” end-to-end. You use AI to work smarter and solve problems faster. Here, you’ll tackle complex, high‑visibility problems with exceptional teammates and grow your career as we build the future of global banking. If that sounds like you, let’s build what’s next.

Growth Data Science Team


The Growth Data Science team sits at the center of Airwallex’s growth engine, turning data and AI into a durable competitive advantage. We build and scale advanced solutions—from predictive modeling and personalization to LLM-powered systems, incentive optimization, and causal inference—to unlock step-change growth across the customer lifecycle.

Learn more about the data science team in this blog.

The Role


We’re seeking a Senior Data Scientist to partner closely with the Growth and Marketing teams to design, deploy, and iterate on high-impact data science solutions that drive measurable business outcomes. In this role, you will apply machine learning models to influence how we acquire, engage, and retain customers globally.

Your work will directly shape Airwallex’s growth strategy, influencing how we acquire, engage, and retain customers. You are naturally curious, business-oriented, and have a proven track record of translating data insights into actionable recommendations that strengthen brand presence and accelerate growth.

Why Join Now


You’ll join the Airwallex team during a critical period, where we are building a team that enables both global consistency in process, tooling and methodology and local flexibility and speed in execution. This is a great opportunity to shape the next phase of Airwallex’s growth.

Responsibilities


Develop predictive models to identify high-value users for Marketing and Commercial teams.

Apply machine learning and/or statistical models to measure causal impact of marketing initiatives.

Develop personalization solutions to drive the client onboarding efficiencies, optimize the referral incentive impacts and also the product cross-selling.

Develop the churn models to drive the growth retention strategy.

Partner with Marketing, Commercial, Engineering, and cross-functional teams to inform, influence, support, and execute GTM strategy and investment decisions.

Qualifications


5+ years industry experience and an advanced degree (PhD or MS) in a quantitative field (e.g. Statistics, Engineering, Sciences, Computer Science, Economics)

Experience with communicating the results of analyses to executives and cross-functional teams to influence the strategy

Expert in machine learning modeling to drive data driven growth strategy

Hands-on experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning models in production-grade systems (e.g., cloud-based services, real-time and batch pipelines).

Expert in data querying languages (e.g. SQL), scripting languages (e.g. Python), experience in schema design and dimensional data modeling a plus

Experience in technology, financial services and/or a high growth environment is advantageous

Applicant Safety Policy: Fraud and Third-Party Recruiters


To protect you from recruitment scams, please be aware that Airwallex will not ask for bank details, sensitive ID numbers (i.e. passport), or any form of payment during the application or interview process. All official communication will come from an @airwallex.com email address. Please apply only through careers.airwallex.com or our official LinkedIn page.

Airwallex does not accept unsolicited resumes from search firms/recruiters. Airwallex will not pay any fees to search firms/recruiters if a candidate is submitted by a search firm/recruiter unless an agreement has been entered into with respect to specific open position(s). Search firms/recruiters submitting resumes to Airwallex on an unsolicited basis shall be deemed to accept this condition, regardless of any other provision to the contrary.

Equal opportunity


Airwallex is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and anyone seeking employment at Airwallex is considered based on merit, qualifications, competence and talent. We don’t regard color, religion, race, national origin, sexual orientation, ancestry, citizenship, sex, marital or family status, disability, gender, or any other legally protected status when making our hiring decisions. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.

This role is for a "behavioral architect"—someone who can look at a spreadsheet of data, understand the human motivation behind the numbers, and design scalable operational systems that drive revenue, viewership, and creator loyalty. New Zealand markets. Responsibilities:

You will design incubation programs that transform raw talent into platform icons.

You will ensure that communication with creators is not just a broadcast, but a feedback loop that fosters belonging and education.

5+ years of experience in creator growth, business development, behavioral strategy, or community scaling.

Analytical Depth: Proficiency in translating business problems into analytical solutions.

You don't just report data; you tell a story with it and provide a solution.

Bias for Action: Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple projects without losing sight of the strategic "big picture."

Preferred Qualifications:

Strategic Empathy: High EQ with a natural ability to mentor and coach creators through their unique challenges.
Trend Spotting: Direct experience in mobile entertainment with an intuitive grasp of emerging trends and ANZ-specific user behaviors.
Communication Mastery: Exceptional cross-functional collaboration skills; able to influence internal stakeholders and external creators with equal ease.

Job Profile Summary

The OCM Senior Analyst plays a key role in designing, leading, and delivering organisational change initiatives that enable leaders, teams, and organisations to successfully adopt changes across business processes, systems, technology, and operating models. Working as a trusted member of the Unisys Organisational Change Management (OCM) team, this role partners closely with OCM Managers, project leadership, and senior stakeholders to drive measurable adoption outcomes.

The Senior OCM Analyst brings strong hands‑on experience, sound judgment, and methodological discipline to change delivery—owning workstreams end‑to‑end, coaching junior team members, and ensuring change activities are aligned to business outcomes, risk management, and benefits realisation.

Key Responsibilities

Change Strategy & Delivery

Lead the design and execution of OCM activities aligned to project objectives, business outcomes, and stakeholder needs.

Conduct and interpret change impact, readiness, and risk assessments at individual, team, and organisational levels.

Develop and tailor integrated change strategies, including communications, engagement, training, and resistance management plans.

Proactively identify change risks and adoption barriers; design mitigation strategies to address resistance and capability gaps.

Track, analyse, and report on adoption metrics, behavioural indicators, and change effectiveness to inform decision‑making.

Contribute to sustainment planning to embed change into business‑as‑usual operations.

Hybrid working arrangement (2-3 days onsite in Eastern Suburbs of Melbourne)

ROLE OVERVIEW
We are seeking a highly analytical IT Business Analyst to drive major technology initiatives. In this role, you will leverage data to prioritize and modernize physical locations requiring critical IT infrastructure upgrades. Additionally, you will assess our broader technology landscape by mapping user lifecycles, evaluating current systems against enterprise architecture, and building strategic roadmaps to optimize the overall experience.

WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Conduct evidence-based research and data analysis to evaluate, prioritize, and select physical locations that require immediate IT capability upgrades.
Evaluate the current technology stack and software applications to ensure they align perfectly with the broader business architecture and operational goals.
Map out existing value chains and operational workflows, with a specific focus on defining and optimizing the high-school student lifecycle.
Drive the end-to-end requirements lifecycle, capturing robust business, user, and functional specifications tailored to business capabilities.
Analyze and reverse-engineer existing legacy systems to uncover current technical capabilities and constraints.
Formulate strategic recommendations based on your analysis and confidently present these insights to the senior leadership team.
Actively support the execution of IT projects, bringing strong delivery skills to the table, and assisting the team with scoping and effort estimation for upcoming work.
Take ownership of key project documentation, producing comprehensive Business Analysis Plans, compelling Business Cases, detailed Student Lifecycle models, and day-to-day analysis reports.
Partner closely with the Project Manager to prepare and deliver robust project governance packs.

MORE ABOUT YOU

Possess a highly consultative mindset, allowing you to untangle complex problems and deliver pragmatic solutions within a fast-paced environment.

Exhibit highly effective communication skills, capable of leading clear, productive discussions with both internal teams and external partners.

A proven track record of top-tier stakeholder management, ensuring all project sponsors, technical teams, and business units are kept aligned and informed.

Have a natural ability to build trust and rapport quickly, particularly with local staff and operational personnel.

Deep experience in enterprise analysis, with the unique ability to reverse-engineer technical systems while keeping the focus firmly on business value and user experience.

At Roche you can show up as yourself, embraced for the unique qualities you bring. The Position

As a Market Access Partner, you are a strategic architect of patient access, securing the pathway for life-changing healthcare by navigating Australia's complex reimbursement systems. Your mission is to drive optimal commercial outcomes, ensuring that innovative medicines and health technologies are reimbursed with favorable clinical labels and within competitive timeframes. You lead lifecycle value management, ensuring the economic and community value of our treatments remains aligned with evolving real-world evidence.

What you will be working on:

As a Market Access Partner you will:

Lead the development and execution of HTA submissions (PBAC/MSAC) to secure and maintain funding for Roche’s innovative portfolio.

Design integrated Access Strategic Plans, aligning TGA regulatory timelines with reimbursement milestones to accelerate patient reach.

Collaborate on advanced pharmaco-economic modeling and budget impact analyses to demonstrate the value of our therapies.

Manage the ongoing value of our products, responding to complex strategic challenges and evolving data throughout the product lifecycle.

Partner with Therapeutic Area Squads and Corporate and Public Affairs to influence the organisation’s long-term portfolio and access strategy.

Pioneer and experiment with sophisticated, value-based access models and innovative funding frameworks.

Participate in and influence Global Access teams, ensuring Australian requirements are integrated into global development plans.

Act as a primary point of contact for external bodies such as the Department of Health, ensuring Roche’s value proposition is clearly understood.

Lead and inspire internal chapters, including Regulatory and Corporate and Public Affairs, to deliver a cohesive access vision.

Lead the generation and synthesis of evidence required to support robust HTA submissions and respond to post-submission reviews.

Who you are:

You are a natural collaborator, an outstanding communicator and influencer who is able to build strong relationships with a diverse range of stakeholders.

You have:

An undergraduate degree in Economics, Pharmacy, Public Health, Mathematics, Statistics, or Life Sciences.

In-depth experience within the pharmaceutical or biotechnology industry, specifically focusing on clinical and economic evaluations.

Proven track record of designing and executing successful market access strategies within the Australian healthcare system.

Technical expertise in Health Technology Assessment (HTA), including economic evaluation and budget impact modeling.

Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with a natural ability to inspire and influence both internal and external stakeholders.

A high degree of strategic agility, allowing you to navigate ambiguity and pivot in a rapidly changing regulatory environment.

A "can-do" and growth-oriented mindset, thriving in agile ways of working where experimentation and risk-taking are encouraged.

The organisational capacity to manage multiple complex projects simultaneously while maintaining a sharp focus on achieving results.

This Sydney-based role involves national and limited  international travel as per the business needs, along with occasional evening and weekend work to meet global timelines. Full eligibility to live and work in Australia is required to be considered for this position.

Ad Text:
The role: 

A Clinical Solution Delivery Analyst plays a critical role in bridging clinical and business needs and IT solutions across eHealth Queensland, providing strategic advice on project direction, facilitate business requirements, use case development, and detailed engagement with clinical and business stakeholders. The role includes mentoring business analysts whilst ensuring effective communication and stakeholder engagement. The focus of this role is to ensure delivered solutions meet business objectives and drive operational efficiency.

Is this opportunity for you?

To perform successfully in this role, you must demonstrate a highly developed ability to manage staff and build client focused and effective teams providing high-quality services to clients in line with contemporary human resource management policies, procedures and practices.

eHealth Queensland is one of the largest Information Communication Technology operations in the state. It is responsible for ensuring the smooth operation of information systems and technologies so that Queensland Health employees and health providers have access to information that supports the delivery of health care.

About Delivery Services Branch - eHealth Queensland:

The Delivery Services Branch is Queensland Health's trusted partner and advisor for digital health project delivery excellence. The branch includes the following units:

Digital Solutions and Technology
Digital Health and Business Solutions
Delivery Support Services
Clinical Program
Digital Partnership Teams
Digitally Enabled Business Solutions
Digital Capital Program

The core function of the branch is to ensure successful delivery of major IT projects and initiatives to our customers across Queensland Health for the benefit of all Queenslanders. This function is critical to eHealth Queensland realising its overall vision, which is to 'advance healthcare through digital innovation'.

As an integral part of our professional project delivery team, each branch team member puts the customer at the centre of everything they do and to actively seek out solutions that will help eHealth Queensland deliver on its targets.

Benefits of working with eHealth Queensland:

By joining eHealth Queensland, you will work within a fast-paced, challenging and supportive environment where every employee plays a critical role in our ongoing success. 

You will enjoy a variety of benefits, including:

Competitive salary + generous superannuation and leave loading
Flexible working arrangements
Diverse work culture
Career training and development
